message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should reject claim when damages contain more entries of a type than insured — two sword damages but only one sword insured"
Expected behavior: claimOffice should throw when damages array has more entries of a type than the policy covers
Current error: "expected [Function] to throw an error" — no validation
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Add minimal validation in the claim processing branch:
1. Count items per type in the policy
2. Count damages per type in the incident
3. If any type has more damages than insured items, throw an Error

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text:
The test is clear: when damages contain more entries of a type than the policy covers, the function should throw. The current claim processing branch has no such validation. I need to add minimal validation in the claim branch.

The minimal implementation: count items per type in the policy, count damages per type in the incident, and throw if any damage type exceeds the insured count.

content.text:
All 40 tests pass, 12 remain as todo.

**Green Phase Complete:**

**Implementation**: Added validation in the claim processing branch that counts items per type in the policy and damages per type in the incident, then throws an Error if any damage type has more entries than insured items.

**Result**: All tests now pass (40 passing, 12 todo).

**Approach**: This is minimal -- the validation counts only what is needed (per-type counts for policy items and damages) and throws immediately when a mismatch is found. No additional logic, no refactoring of existing code. The validation is placed right before the payout loop so it short-circuits before any processing occurs.

**File modified**: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-4/src/claim-office.ts` (lines 34-44, added count-and-compare validation).

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
